---
title: "diabetes_modeling_korea"
format: html
editor: visual
---

### Libraries

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(rriskDistributions)
library(writexl)
```

## Importing data

```{r}
# loading danish inputs, sex, LOD, BPS percentiles & diabetes incidence
# maybe name it better :-)
# doing lognormal distribution to the danish percentiles
df <- read_excel("diabetes work china-kopi.xlsx") %>%
  mutate(
    meanlog_dk = log(p_50),
    sdlog_dk   = (log(p_75) - log(p_25)) / (qnorm(0.75) - qnorm(0.25)),
    # exepected fraction of population above LOD
    p_detectable_dk = 1 - plnorm(LOD, meanlog_dk, sdlog_dk),
    # arithemtic mean
    # is this right?
    mean_exp_dk = exp(meanlog_dk + 0.5 * sdlog_dk^2), # not used right now, PAF too high
    median_dk = p_50 # currently used
  )
```

## Korean study inputs, BPS and diabetes

```{r}
# Supplementary Table S6: DM ORs among participants with BPS > LOD
OR_t2  <- 1.73
LCL_t2 <- 1.00
UCL_t2 <- 2.98

OR_t3  <- 2.13
LCL_t3 <- 1.29
UCL_t3 <- 3.50

# Convert 95% CI to log-scale SE
# simulating uncertainty
se_t2 <- (log(UCL_t2) - log(LCL_t2)) / (2 * 1.96)
se_t3 <- (log(UCL_t3) - log(LCL_t3)) / (2 * 1.96)

```

```{r}
# Supplementary Table S1: Korean detectable BPS distribution (> LOD)
# Using p25, p50, and p75 to fit a lognormal distribution

fit_kor <- get.lnorm.par(
  p = c(0.25, 0.50, 0.75),
  q = c(0.029, 0.049, 0.104),
  show.output = FALSE,
  plot = TRUE
)

# extract the fitted lognormal parameters as ordinary numbers
meanlog_kor <- unname(fit_kor["meanlog"])
sdlog_kor   <- unname(fit_kor["sdlog"])

# Simulate Korean detectable exposure distribution
set.seed(123)
x_kor <- rlnorm(1e6, meanlog = meanlog_kor, sdlog = sdlog_kor)

# Tertile cutpoints among detectable Korean values
cut1 <- qlnorm(1/3, meanlog = meanlog_kor, sdlog = sdlog_kor)
cut2 <- qlnorm(2/3, meanlog = meanlog_kor, sdlog = sdlog_kor)

# Medians within each tertile
# finding the typical dose within the tertile
# cuts represent the boundaries
dose_t1 <- median(x_kor[x_kor <= cut1])
dose_t2 <- median(x_kor[x_kor > cut1 & x_kor <= cut2])
dose_t3 <- median(x_kor[x_kor > cut2])


## Build dose-response dataset and fit linear trend ##
# dose = representative exposures (medians) for the 3 tertiles
# dose_c = making tertile 1 the reference point/baseline where log(RR)=0, RR=1
# IS dose_c NECESSARY?
# logrr = converting the RR to the log scale
# se = using the SE for the log-risk estimates
study_dat <- tibble(
  dose   = c(dose_t1, dose_t2, dose_t3),
  dose_c = c(dose_t1, dose_t2, dose_t3) - dose_t1,
  logrr  = log(c(1, OR_t2, OR_t3)),
  se     = c(NA, se_t2, se_t3)
)


# fitting linear model, trying to finde the slope beta of the best straight line
# lm(logrr ~ 0 + dose_c) fits the line without an intercept to force it through (0,0) 
# this means dose_c = 0, logrr = 0 which makes 1 at reference dose
# data = study_dat[-1, ], uses just tertile 2 and 3 (removes the first row) because tertile 1 is reference

fit_lin <- lm(logrr ~ 0 + dose_c,
              data = study_dat[2:3, ],
              weights = 1 / study_dat$se[2:3]^2) #smaller SE -> larger weight -> more influence

# estimated slope of line + uncertainty of the estimated slope
beta_hat <- unname(coef(fit_lin)["dose_c"])
beta_se  <- summary(fit_lin)$coefficients["dose_c", "Std. Error"]

# reference dose
x_ref <- dose_t1


beta_hat
beta_se
summary(fit_lin)
study_dat
```

## Duration inputs for YLD

```{r}
# Median age at onset of type 2 diabetes in DK
age_m <- 62.1
age_f <- 64.6

# Life expectancy in DK
life_m <- 78.8
life_f <- 82.8

# estimating the duration
D_m <- life_m - age_m
D_f <- life_f - age_f

# adding to dataframe
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    D_years = case_when(
      str_detect(group, "^Male")   ~ D_m,
      str_detect(group, "^Female") ~ D_f,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  )
```

## Inputs for YLL

```{r}
# deaths and diabetes from GBD (DK + T2D)
deaths_f <- 566.89
deaths_m <- 715.34

yll_f <- 7837.82
yll_m <- 12702.54

# calculating standard expected years of life lost
seyll_f <- yll_f / deaths_f
seyll_m <- yll_m / deaths_m

# adding to dataframe
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    deaths = case_when(
      str_detect(group, "^Female") ~ deaths_f,
      str_detect(group, "^Male")   ~ deaths_m,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    seyll = case_when(
      str_detect(group, "^Female") ~ seyll_f,
      str_detect(group, "^Male")   ~ seyll_m,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  )
```

## Disability weight inputs

```{r}
# Uncomplicated diabetes mellitus type 2
DW_mode <- 0.049011415
DW_min  <- 0.0311213425
DW_max  <- 0.072092547
```

## Helper functions

```{r}
# PERT distribution for disability weight uncertainty
rpert <- function(n, min, mode, max) {
  alpha <- 1 + 4 * (mode - min) / (max - min)
  beta  <- 1 + 4 * (max - mode) / (max - min)
  min + rbeta(n, alpha, beta) * (max - min)
}

```

## Monte Carlo 

```{r}
run_mc_row_daly_korea_simple <- function(
  i_diabetes, median_dk, p_detectable_dk,
  D_years, deaths, seyll,
  DW_min, DW_mode, DW_max,
  beta_hat, beta_se, x_ref,
  baseline_uncertainty = FALSE
) {


  # uncertainty in slope
  # draw one possible value of the dose-response slope from a normal distribution centered at the estimated slope
  beta <- rnorm(1, mean = beta_hat, sd = beta_se)

  # RR at average detectable Danish exposure, calculate the RR for a person whose exposure is the average detectable Danish exposure
  # Danish detectable average exposure - Korean reference dose
  # log(RR) = beta * (dose - reference dose)
  rr_mean <- exp(beta * (median_dk - x_ref))

  # PAF among detectable
  PAF_det <- (rr_mean - 1) / rr_mean

  # scale to full population but stick to the fraction of detectable BPS in population
  PAF <- p_detectable_dk * PAF_det

  base_cases <- if (baseline_uncertainty) {
    rpois(1, lambda = i_diabetes)
  } else {
    i_diabetes
  }

  # Attributable cases
  AC <- PAF * base_cases

  # draw a disability weight (is also uncertain, therefore PERT)
  DW <- rpert(1, DW_min, DW_mode, DW_max)

  # years lived with disability
  YLD <- AC * D_years * DW
  # attributable deaths
  AD  <- PAF * deaths
  # years of life lost
  YLL <- AD * seyll
  
  DALY <- YLD + YLL

  # return results
  tibble(
    PAF = PAF,
    AC = AC,
    AD = AD,
    YLD = YLD,
    YLL = YLL,
    DALY = DALY
  )
}
```

```{r}
run_mc_summary_daly_korea_simple <- function(
  i_diabetes, median_dk, p_detectable_dk,
  D_years, deaths, seyll,
  DW_min, DW_mode, DW_max,
  beta_hat, beta_se, x_ref,
  nsim_outer = 3000,
  baseline_uncertainty = FALSE
) {

  sims <- map_dfr(seq_len(nsim_outer), ~
    run_mc_row_daly_korea_simple(
      i_diabetes = i_diabetes,
      median_dk = median_dk,
      p_detectable_dk = p_detectable_dk,
      D_years = D_years,
      deaths = deaths,
      seyll = seyll,
      DW_min = DW_min,
      DW_mode = DW_mode,
      DW_max = DW_max,
      beta_hat = beta_hat,
      beta_se = beta_se,
      x_ref = x_ref,
      baseline_uncertainty = baseline_uncertainty
    )
  )

  tibble(
    PAF_med  = median(sims$PAF, na.rm = TRUE),
    PAF_lo   = quantile(sims$PAF, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    PAF_hi   = quantile(sims$PAF, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),

    AC_med   = median(sims$AC, na.rm = TRUE),
    AC_lo    = quantile(sims$AC, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    AC_hi    = quantile(sims$AC, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),

    AD_med   = median(sims$AD, na.rm = TRUE),
    AD_lo    = quantile(sims$AD, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    AD_hi    = quantile(sims$AD, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),

    YLD_med  = median(sims$YLD, na.rm = TRUE),
    YLD_lo   = quantile(sims$YLD, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    YLD_hi   = quantile(sims$YLD, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),

    YLL_med  = median(sims$YLL, na.rm = TRUE),
    YLL_lo   = quantile(sims$YLL, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    YLL_hi   = quantile(sims$YLL, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),

    DALY_med = median(sims$DALY, na.rm = TRUE),
    DALY_lo  = quantile(sims$DALY, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    DALY_hi  = quantile(sims$DALY, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}
```

```{r}
results_daly_korea_simple <- df %>%
  mutate(
    mc = pmap(
      list(i_diabetes, median_dk, p_detectable_dk, D_years, deaths, seyll),
      ~ run_mc_summary_daly_korea_simple(
        i_diabetes = ..1,
        median_dk = ..2,
        p_detectable_dk = ..3,
        D_years = ..4,
        deaths = ..5,
        seyll = ..6,
        DW_min = DW_min,
        DW_mode = DW_mode,
        DW_max = DW_max,
        beta_hat = beta_hat,
        beta_se = beta_se,
        x_ref = x_ref,
        nsim_outer = 3000,
        baseline_uncertainty = FALSE
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  unnest(mc)

results_daly_korea_simple
```

```{r}
getwd()
#write_xlsx(results_daly_korea, "results_diabetes_korea.xlsx")
```

## Checking percentiles

```{r}
# Original Korean target percentiles
target_kor <- tibble(
  percentile = c("p25", "p50", "p75"),
  target = c(0.029, 0.049, 0.104)
)

# Back-calculated percentiles from fitted lognormal
check_kor <- tibble(
  percentile = c("p25", "p50", "p75"),
  fitted = qlnorm(c(0.25, 0.50, 0.75), meanlog_kor, sdlog_kor)
)

# Compare
compare_kor <- target_kor %>%
  left_join(check_kor, by = "percentile") %>%
  mutate(
    abs_diff = fitted - target,
    rel_diff_pct = 100 * (fitted - target) / target
  )

compare_kor

# qlnorm() gives you the quantile of a lognormal distribution

# target = the original percentile
# fitted = the percentiles from the fitted lognormal
# abs_diff = fitted - target
# rel_diff_pct = percent difference realtive to target

# looks like a reasonable fit
# not reproducing the percentiles perfectly, but deviations are small enough to be accepted
# imo
```

## Checks

```{r}
beta_hat
x_ref
df %>%
  mutate(rr_at_median = exp(beta_hat * (median_dk - x_ref))) %>%
  select(group, median_dk, rr_at_median)

# is beta too steep?
# rr is high
```
